Test Description: Register default-value verification and masked write/read compliance across the address list defined for the GPIO block.
Remarks: Addresses with read_mask_array[i]==0 or write_mask_array[i]==0 are explicitly skipped; addresses flagged in skip_rst_array[]/skip_array[] are skipped.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Environment and setup:
- Use arrays and constants from test_define.c: addr_array[], default_value_array[], read_mask_array[], write_mask_array[], skip_rst_array[], skip_array[], and CNT (count of registers).
Initialization:
- Reset failure counters for default-value mismatches and write-read mismatches to zero.
Default-value verification (for each index i from 0 to CNT-1):
- Determine the register address from addr_array[i].
- If skip_rst_array[i] equals 1, skip this register.
- If read_mask_array[i] equals 0x00000000, skip reading for this register.
- Read the register and apply a mask of 0xFFFFFFFE to the read value.
- Compare the masked read value against default_value_array[i]; record a default-value failure on mismatch.
Patterned writes (six patterns in this order): 0xFFFFFFFF, 0xAAAAAAAA, 0x55555555, 0xF5F5F5F5, 0xA5A5A5A5, 0xFFFF0000.
- For each pattern and for each index i from 0 to CNT-1:
  - Determine the register address from addr_array[i].
  - If skip_array[i] equals 1, skip this register.
  - If write_mask_array[i] equals 0x00000000, skip writing for this register.
  - Write the pattern masked by write_mask_array[i] to the register.
Readback and verification (same index order):
- For each index i from 0 to CNT-1:
  - If skip_array[i] equals 1, skip this register.
  - If write_mask_array[i] equals 0x00000000 or read_mask_array[i] equals 0x00000000, skip verification for this register.
  - Read the register and apply read_mask_array[i] to the value.
  - Derive the expected value by combining: bits written by the current pattern limited by both read_mask_array[i] and write_mask_array[i], plus the preserved default bits limited by read_mask_array[i] where the write mask is zero.
  - If the read value does not match the expected value, record a write-read failure.
Cleanup and termination:
- If any default-value or write-read failures were recorded, terminate with failure; otherwise, terminate with success.
Validation / Acceptance Criteria: PASS when both counters are zero at termination (no default-value mismatches and no write-read mismatches). Any recorded mismatch leads to failure.
Gap Analysis: No negative tests (e.g., invalid addresses) and no error-retry strategy; registers marked non-readable/non-writable are skipped without alternative verification.

Test Description: Negative-edge interrupt enable and handling for GPIO pads 8–39; verifies per-pin enable, group status set, per-pin/raw clear, and system IRQ clear.
Remarks: Conditional build selects GPIO0 (IRQ 87) or GPIO1 (IRQ 88) paths; per-pin configuration uses bit positions 20, 18, and 16; uses int_pend as a run-loop gate with a timeout of 5000 iterations.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Environment and setup:
- Enable platform interrupt: IRQ 87 if GPIO0 is defined; IRQ 88 if GPIO1 is defined.
- Enable system-register output by writing MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 with either LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR or LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR.
Initialization and configuration:
- Drive all GPIO pads high by writing 0xFFFFFFFF to address 0xA0243FFC.
- For each pad index i from 0 to 31 (corresponding to pads 8–39):
  - Program the per-pad register at (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + i*4) to set bit20, bit18, and bit16 to 1.
  - Delay 10 time units after each configuration write.
Per-pad stimulus and arming (loop i from 0 to 31):
- Prepare a one-hot mask for the selected pad (bit i).
- Clear the per-pad raw status by writing the mask to MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1.
- Enable the pad’s group interrupt by writing the same mask to MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1; delay 10 time units.
- Arm the run-loop gate by setting int_pend to 1.
- Generate a falling edge for the selected pad by first writing 0xFFFFFFFF to 0xA0243FFC, delaying 30 time units, and then writing a value with bit i cleared and all other bits set to 1 to 0xA0243FFC.
Runtime handling and timeout:
- Poll int_pend with a timeout counter initialized to 5000; in each poll, delay 10 time units and decrement the counter.
- If the counter reaches zero before int_pend clears, record a timeout error for that pad.
Interrupt service routine behavior (Default_IRQHandler):
- Clear the run-loop gate by setting int_pend to 0.
- Restore all pads high by writing 0xFFFFFFFF to 0xA0243FFC.
- Read the per-pad register at (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + i*4) and verify:
  - Bit0 equals 0; any non-zero indicates error.
  - Bit1 equals 1; if set, read MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 and ensure the bit corresponding to pad i is set; otherwise, record an error.
- Reprogram the per-pad register at (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + i*4) to set bit20 and bit16 to 1 (clears posedge enable while keeping configuration consistent).
- Clear the per-pad raw status by writing the one-hot mask for pad i to MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1.
- Verify the group status register MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 becomes 0 after the clear; any non-zero indicates error.
- Clear the system raw interrupt by writing the appropriate bit (LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR or LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR) to MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, and clear the platform IRQ (87 or 88).
Cleanup and termination:
- After all pads are exercised, terminate with success if no errors were recorded; otherwise, terminate with failure.
Impacted Registers: MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1, MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, 0xA0243FFC
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- No timeouts during polling for any pad (32 iterations).
- In the ISR: per-pad status bit1 is set on entry; the corresponding group status bit is set on entry and becomes 0 after raw clear.
- System raw interrupt in MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 is cleared; platform IRQ (87 or 88) is cleared.
- Overall: error counter remains 0 at termination.
Gap Analysis: No explicit negative tests (e.g., spurious or simultaneous interrupts); no debounce/noise robustness checks; relies on a shared iteration index i inside the ISR without concurrency protection.

Test Description: Positive-edge interrupt enable across all pads 8–39; verifies group status set, raw-per-pin clear, sysreg clear, and re-enable for next iteration.
Remarks: Conditional build selects GPIO0 (IRQ 87) or GPIO1 (IRQ 88); per-pad posedge enable uses 0x00020000; int_pend is a volatile gate with a timeout of 2000 iterations.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Environment and setup:
- Enable platform interrupt: IRQ 87 if GPIO0 is defined; IRQ 88 if GPIO1 is defined.
- Enable system-register output by writing MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 with either LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR or LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR.
Configuration:
- For each pad index i from 0 to 31 (pads 8–39), enable positive-edge detection by writing 0x00020000 to (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + i*4).
- Delay 10 time units.
- Configure IO control groups for input mode by writing 0x000000FF to each of: MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1, GROUP2, GROUP3, and GROUP4.
- Delay 10 time units.
- Enable group interrupt output by writing 0xFFFFFFFF to MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1.
Stimulus per iteration (loop i from 0 to 31):
- Drive all pads low by writing 0x00000000 to 0xA0243FFC; delay 10 time units.
- Arm the run-loop gate by setting int_pend to 1.
- Generate a rising edge by writing 0xFFFFFFFF to 0xA0243FFC.
Runtime handling and timeout:
- Poll int_pend with a timeout set to 2000 iterations; in each poll, delay 10 time units.
- On timeout expiry, record an error and stop further iterations.
- After servicing, drive pads low again (0x00000000 to 0xA0243FFC) and delay 10 time units to prepare for the next iteration.
Interrupt service routine behavior (Default_IRQHandler):
- Form a one-hot mask for the current pad (bit i); clear the run-loop gate by setting int_pend to 0.
- Read the group status register MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 and temporarily mask group output by writing 0x00000000 to MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1.
- If the group status is non-zero on entry, treat as success; otherwise, record an error.
- Clear raw per-pad status for all pads by writing 0x00010000 to each per-pad register at (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + j*4) for j from 0 to 31; delay 2 time units.
- Verify that MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 equals 0 after raw clears; record an error if not.
- Clear the system raw interrupt in MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 using the appropriate bit (GPIO0 or GPIO1); read back the same register and record an error if the bit remains set.
- Re-enable group interrupt output by writing 0xFFFFFFFF to MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1; clear platform IRQ (87 or 88).
Cleanup and termination:
- After iterating through all 32 pads, terminate with success if no errors were recorded; otherwise, terminate with failure.
Impacted Registers: MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP2, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP3, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP4, MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, 0xA0243FFC
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- No timeout events occur during any of the 32 iterations.
- Group interrupt status (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) is non-zero on entry to the ISR and becomes 0 after raw clears.
- System raw status in MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 clears and remains cleared after readback; platform IRQ is cleared.
- Overall: the error counter equals 0 at termination.
Gap Analysis: No explicit negative tests (e.g., spurious triggers, simultaneous edges); raw clear is broadcast to all pads without per-pad isolation check; no debounce/noise robustness checks.
